tool_name: Mass Concrete Volume, Thermal Crack Risk & Pour Cost (ACI 207)

inputs.name: Cement Content per m³
inputs.hint: Lean concrete: 150–200 | Standard C25: 280–320 | High strength C45: 380–450

inputs.name: Cement Type / Heat Class
options: CEM III/B (GGBS ≥66%) — Very low heat: factor 0.55

inputs.name: Cement Heat Generation Factor
inputs.hint: From cement type selection: CEM I=1.00 | CEM III/B=0.55

inputs.name: Ambient Air Temperature at Pour
inputs.hint: Use forecast temperature for the pour date and first 72 hours

inputs.name: Minimum Section Thickness
inputs.hint: ACI 207.1R: Mass concrete defined as thickness ≥ 900mm (36 in)

formulas: Gross_Volume_m3 = vol_slab_m3 + vol_footing_m3 + vol_column_m3   // [m³] Total geometric volume
Rebar_Displacement_m3 = Gross_Volume_m3 * (rebar_volume_pct / 100)   // [m³] Volume displaced by reinforcement
Net_Concrete_m3 = Gross_Volume_m3 - Rebar_Displacement_m3   // [m³] Net concrete volume required
Order_Volume_m3 = Net_Concrete_m3 * (1 + pour_waste_pct / 100)   // [m³] Order quantity incl. waste
Total_Material_Cost_USD = Order_Volume_m3 * concrete_unit_cost_usd   // [USD] Concrete supply cost
Total_Pour_Cost_USD = Total_Material_Cost_USD + pump_mobilization_usd   // [USD] Total placement cost
Cost_per_Net_m3_USD = Total_Pour_Cost_USD / Net_Concrete_m3   // [USD/m³] All-in unit cost
Heat_of_Hydration_kJ_kg = 350 * cement_heat_factor   // [kJ/kg] Cement heat release — ACI 207.2R Table 3.1
Adiabatic_Temp_Rise_C = (cement_content_kg_m3 * Heat_of_Hydration_kJ_kg) / (2400 * 0.92)   // [°C] Max adiabatic temperature rise | ACI 207.1R
Peak_Core_Temp_C = ambient_temp_c + Adiabatic_Temp_Rise_C   // [°C] Estimated peak core temperature
Surface_Temp_C = ambient_temp_c + 5   // [°C] Approximate surface temperature (simplified — no insulation)
Delta_T_Core_Surface = Peak_Core_Temp_C - Surface_Temp_C   // [°C] Thermal gradient — crack driver
Concrete_Weight_tonnes = Net_Concrete_m3 * 2.4   // [tonnes] Structural weight (reinforced concrete density)

total_volume_positive.error_msg: Total concrete volume is zero. Enter at least one non-zero volume (slab, footing, or column).

cement_content_range.error_msg: Cement content < 150 kg/m³: Below minimum for structural concrete workability and strength. ACI 318 minimum for structural applications: 250 kg/m³.

mass_concrete_threshold.error_msg: MASS CONCRETE CONDITIONS: Section ≥ 900mm with high-heat cement (CEM I/II). ACI 207.1R mass concrete protocols mandatory. Thermal control plan required before pouring.

smart_warnings.message: THERMAL CRACKING RISK CONFIRMED: Core-to-surface ΔT exceeds 20°C threshold. Restrained thermal shrinkage will generate tensile stresses exceeding concrete tensile strength. Through-cracking expected. Mandatory mitigations: (1) Use CEM III/B or pozzolanic cement, (2) Pre-cool mix water or aggregate, (3) Limit pour lifts to 600mm, (4) Apply thermal insulation blanket to retain surface heat.

SEVERE THERMAL CRACKING — ΔT > 25°C: At this differential, macro-cracks of 0.2–0.5mm width are near-certain. Structure integrity and watertightness compromised. Redesign concrete mix with low-heat cement and thermal monitoring sensors (embedded thermocouples) required.

HOT WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient temperature > 32°C. Concrete may set prematurely. ACI 305R requirements: (1) Fresh concrete temperature ≤ 35°C at point of discharge, (2) Use chilled water or ice substitution, (3) Pour at night, (4) Increase slump via plasticizer — not water.

COLD WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient < 5°C. Hydration will stall or freeze. ACI 306R: Fresh concrete must be maintained at ≥ 10°C for 7 days minimum. Insulated formwork, heated enclosure, or anti-freeze admixtures required.

DELAYED ETTRINGITE FORMATION (DEF) RISK: Peak core temperature > 70°C. Ettringite phase destroyed during curing and reforms later causing expansive cracking. Use water-reducing admixtures and low-C₃A cements. This risk is irreversible once temperature is exceeded.

Waste allowance < 3%: Likely underestimated for construction site conditions. Insufficient waste factor leads to under-ordering, schedule delays, and cold joint formation. Minimum 5% recommended for poured-in-place work.
